Bad Times at the El Royale (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,839,115Details
International Box Office $13,339,827
Worldwide Box Office $31,178,942
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,939,302
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,298,727
Total $5,238,029
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Seven strangers, each with a secret to bury, meet at Lake Tahoe’s El Royale, a rundown hotel with a dark past. Over the course of one fateful night, everyone will have a last shot at redemption… before everything goes to hell.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,132,647 (40.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.50 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:57.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$32,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,808 opening theaters/2,808 max. theaters, 2.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $23,419,952

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 12th, 2018 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: September 27th, 2018 (Wide), released as Hotel de Criminales (Mexico)
October 12th, 2018 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
October 12th, 2018 (Wide) (Germany)
October 12th, 2018 (Wide) (Poland)
October 12th, 2018 (Wide), released as Sunkūs laikai viešbutyje "El Royale" (Lithuania)
Video Release: January 1st, 2019 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong violence, language, some drug content and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2546 (Cert #51720), 9/26/2018)
Running Time: 141 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, One Location, Prologue
Geographical setting: California, Nevada
Plot point: Addiction, False Identity, Faulty Memory, Heist, Motel, Narcotics, Non-Chronological, One Night, Redemption, Singers, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Cults, Film Noir
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Fox, TSG Entertainment, Goddard Textiles
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
